text.text: This page try to summarize all the  projects I have worked on from my beginning in computer science. Of course the first projects are simplistic ... or stupid... But this timeline will show all the aspects I have touched during the last 25 year of computing.

text.headline: Nostalgy: Amstrad CPC (Basic)
text.text: My first PC.

text.headline: Nostalgy: What's your age ?
text.text: My first program in basic, asking for your age and .... saying that you are old or not ! Whaooo.

text.headline: Nostalgy: Circle
text.text: Not really a project, but one of the program I have rewritten from a book many many times. It uses cosinus/sinus to draw a circle, but at that time I didn't learn about this at school (I was 10 years old) so it was magic to me and was fascinated by this !

text.headline: Basic Math Tool
text.text: My first real program, it helps to compute surface and perimeter of selected geometric form, following what I was learning at school.

text.headline: Basic Monopoly Text
text.text: My first game, implementation of a monopoly in text mode. It was not really usable, the board was never drawn, the menu was in text only. I never really finished it. But it was a nice try !

text.headline: Nostalgy: Amstrad PC1512
text.text: A nice step forward from 8 KHz to 8 MHz. Discovery of DOS and windows 1.1. Bad luck I had no programing tool on this PC.

text.headline: Virus script on DOS
text.text: Just a stupid script which inserts itself on boot command and degrades the machine at each boot until nothing works. Was used only on my own PC.... I was reinstalling everything a lot of time to test it !

text.headline: Nostalgy: PIII +  Win ME
text.text: First brand new PC, Pentium III, 64 Mo of RAM, equipped with the totally buggy Windows ME. Still no programming tools....

text.headline: Nostalgy: Ti-83
text.text: Return to basic with my first programmable calculator.

text.headline: Nostalgy: Internet 56K
text.text: First connection to the internet with Wanadoo 56K modem. First mails on Outlook !

text.headline: Ti-83 battleship
text.text: One of my biggest program for Ti-83, battleship playable in network by using cable between two calculators.

text.headline: Ti-83 fight
text.text: You have to fight against the computer with weapons and bomb on various terrains.

text.headline: Ti-Net V1
text.text: First website hosted by Wanadoo to distribute my ti-83 programs. Was the time of flashy colors.... horrible from today rules !

text.headline: Ti-Net V2
text.text: Progress with nice menu but still flashy yellow colors....

text.headline: Nostalgy: Visual Basic 6
text.text: This was my first real development envrionnement, I got it from a friend and being hacked. I learned a lot on programing on it.

text.headline: Cyrpt Einstein
text.text: A cryptographic software totally stupid I made for fun. This was my first real program in Visual Basic and my first work with installers on Windows.

text.headline: simugene
text.text: A gene propagation in population simulator following the lectures in biology. It was written in Visual Basic.

text.headline: Meta Edit
text.text: A GUI to handle meta-tag in web pages. It was implemented in Visual Basic.

text.headline: Ti-Net V3
text.text: This time a real theme based to Ti calculators.

text.headline: Nostalgy: Linux Mandrake
text.text: First step under Linux with a CD I bought in a magazine (internet was too slow to download with limited monthly data). 3 month of try before succeeding in making my modem working. Had to recompile the related kernel module.

text.headline: audiomix
text.text: An audio mixer with multiple tracks and a nice GUI in Visual Basic. It provided a mix table to control volume in real time and record levels for exportation. It was handline WAV files as input and output.

text.headline: Driving log
text.text: Log of supervised driving for myself. Also made in Visual Basic.

text.headline: svlib
text.text: For fun and to learn, rewrite my own C++ library : strings, list, vector...

text.headline: progranet
text.text: Last version of my website, this time it hosts my TI programs plus my Visual Basic projects and some tutorial on programming.

text.headline: svreg
text.text: For fun, tried to implement my own regular expression parsor in C++. Was not totally successful.

text.headline: Nostalgy: building my first PC, and Gentoo
text.text: Building my first PC with AMD64, Nvidia Gforce 6800 LE (unlocking the disabled pipeline to make it like a GT). Moving from Mandrake to Gentoo, only available distribution in 64 bit for free. Building my first Linux Kernel on the fresh new x86_64 platform !

text.headline: emptd-qt
text.text: Custom project made for my first year at university to balance the unreadable excel timetable provided by university. My first real program in QT and C++.

text.headline: emptd-php
text.text: Custom project made for my first year at university to balance the unreadable excel timetable provided by university.It was used by many student from my promotion at the end of the year. It was written in PHP. Following this project the university invested during the summer to build their own version for all the timetable and all students.

text.headline: tutocpp
text.text:
C++ tutorial for Cnet association. We started from list and image manipulation implementation. We then finished by the implementation of a widget management system in SDL and added console rendering in ASCII art. We provided the text box component.

The project is hosted on Github

text.headline: gravit
text.text: A numerical simulation done for bachelor. It simulates the impact of an asteroid on a surface composed of solid particles.

text.headline: cnet-tools
text.text:
Tools developed for the Cnet association at the University of Savoy. It contains:

  • A website
  • A quota control system for cups
  • Network traffic tracking to detect movie downloads on the association network.
  • Firewall rules

text.headline: Cortex
text.text: Cluster based open MOSIX with four nodes built at Cnet association in 2007-2008. We also used Xen as hyperviser. It was financed by university.

text.headline: TSA : Track Svn Admin
text.text: Project used at Cnet for two years to handle svn and trac repository. It was the same idea as gitlab but, of course, largely simpler and developed in perl (I will never redo such a project in perl....).

text.headline: gvDvdRip
text.text: Starting to implement a GUI to RIP DVDs with mencoder. Made in extreme programming with Roland Gaspard using gobby and Skype. We never really finished this project.

text.headline: vzsimu6809
text.text: For one of our physics lab we had to program a microprocessor Motorola 6809 to control an oscilloscope. In order to prepare the session, we, with Julien Zoubian, implemented an emulator linked to a simulated oscilloscope. Hence we were able to prepare our code before the session. The extra goal we wanted (unachieved) was to print the face of the teacher on the oscilloscope. The emulator came with an assembler implemented in perl.

text.headline: crackmd5
text.text: Master project to implement rainbow tables to crackmd5. We used SSE in our C++ implementation to compute multiple sum in parallel. Made with Romain Bouleis.

text.headline: svMontage
text.text: My father made a long time ago a slide show with two projectors controlled by a magneto scope. We wanted to convert it to numeric video. We scanned all the diapositives, dump the control signal and used this software to reassemble the video. It generates a YUV stream to be sent to mencoder. It supported HD video.

text.headline: tprsa-m2stic
text.text: Master project at the University of Savoy to implement RSA protocol.Made with Romain Bouleis.

text.headline: Chat.Net
text.text: Master 2 project to implement a chat in C# .Net at the University of Savoy. Made with Romain Bouleis.

text.headline: S&R Hotels
text.text: Master project from the University of Savoy. The goal was to manage room reservation in J2EE and hibernate. Was done with Romain Bouleis.

text.headline: Tchoutchou modeling
text.text: Master exercises to model (all UMLs) a train simulator.

text.headline: VPN spoofing
text.text: For our cryptographic lecture we had to give a talk on security. With Romain Bouleis we chose to hack the VPN of the Wi-Fi connection of our university. It revealed a mistake in the server configuration permitting to fake the Wi-Fi spot and the VPN server. We were able to capture students login and password. The issue was present at three university using the same configuration : Chambery, Grenoble and Lyon.

text.headline: Virtual Huge Pages
text.text: For my master internship at CEA, I implemented a kernel module to support huge pages and page coloring in order to improve cache usage efficiency.

text.headline: KAudioConverter
text.text:
A GUI to extract audio files from video using KDE library for the GUI. The backend was based on mencoder/mplayer. This was the first project for which I made packaging in RPM/DEB.

The project is hosted on assembla

text.headline: HomeLinux
text.text:
It provides a source package manager for your home directory. The idea is the use the same approach as Gentoo but to build a prefix into your home without rebuilding the dependencies provided by the host system. It started during my PhD. due to lack of up-to-date Linux distribution available in our service. At that time we succeed to install a full KDE on an old centos.

The project is hosted on Github

text.headline: svpgm2txt
text.text:
Project of text recognition into images to extract DVD subtitles. It considered perfect character representation hence it requires less human intervention than gocr. At some point I tried to implement a kind of neural network to auto-determine the unknown characters from a database.

The project is hosted on Github

text.headline: MPC_Allocator
text.text:
For my PhD. I implemented a parallel memory allocator explicitly supporting NUMA architectures for the MPC project from CEA.

The code is embedded into MPC

text.headline: svUnitTest
text.text:
C++ unit test library, similar to CPPunit. It was just before the boom of google unit tests, got some ideas similar to it. This is the first project for which I worked on portability over many systems and compilers : Gentoo, Ubuntu, Fedora, FreeBSD. It was also my second step in unit testing and test driven development.

The code can be found on Github

text.headline: Kernel patch PLPC
text.text: Process Local Page Cache is an experimental patch I implemented for Linux kernel 2.6.26 to avoid memory clearing in the critical path of page faults

text.headline: LBM
text.text: A numerical simulation in MPI + (OpenMP or CUDA) based on the Lattice Bolzmann Method (LBM) to solve the Karman street vortices. It was made for a lab courses in master at University of Versailles. The student had to optimize out suboptimal version.

text.headline: ctags2uml
text.text: UML diagram generation from C++ source code (parsing by ctags). The project provides a converter from CTAGS to GRAPHVIZ. The nice thing compared to existing tools is the ability to automatically based class groups by looking on the links, this help to get a nice layout automatically and not a big mess.

text.headline: htopml
text.text:
A simple tool like htop but with HTML gui. Can work with slurm and can also spy application internals. It had the nice feature to provide a module to display thread binding on topology. New modules can easily be implemented to export applications information.

The code can be found on Github

text.headline: MPC_Allocator_Cpp
text.text:
A C++ rewrite (prototype not finished) of MPC_Allocator I developed during my PhD.

The code can be found on Github

text.headline: Cartesian Mesh Runtime
text.text:
Domain Specific Language based on Latex to implement parallel (OpenMP + MPI) simulation on Cartesian mesh. The idea is to use an abstract language and generate a parallel program plus a detail report on the equations used in the code.

The code can be found on Github

.

text.headline: MALT: A Malloc Tracker
text.text:
For my first postdoc I developed a memory profiling tool showing where the code allocate the memory and how. It provides source annotation as well as time charts.

The project is hosted on Github

text.headline: DAQPIPE
text.text:
As a postdoc at CERN for the LHCb experiment I developed a simulation tool to evaluate capability to build a 40 Tb/s data acquisition system based on InfiniBand or Omni-Path. This application has to scale up to 500 nodes for the core part and send data to 4000 filter nodes.

The project is hosted on CERN Gitlab

text.headline: DAQPIPE-GO
text.text: Starting implementation of DAQPIPE in GO, done a big part of the work then take back the cleaner structure into the C++ verson. Never really finished the GO version but it was nice.

text.headline: numaprof
text.text:
Developed on my free time during my postdoc at CERN, it provides the same approach as MALT with source annotation but to provide information on NUMA memory accesses. It instrument the binary by using pintool.

The project is hosted on Github

text.headline: rust-puzzle-solver
text.text:
This was a side project to learn rust. The idea is to solve jigsaw puzzles by taking picture and building rules to finish it quicly by parts. This was more a trial I didn't yet finished and didn't fully succeed to make working. But it was fun to learn rust !

The non finished project is hosted on Github

text.headline: hpc_allocator_rust
text.text:
Developed on my free time during my postdoc at CERN. The project is a reimplementation in Rust of the NUMA memory allocator I implemented during my PhD. at CEA. The idea is to evaluate rust for low level system programming and check performance compared to C implementation by knowing it produce safer and easier to manage code.

The project still in progress is hosted on Github

text.headline: ATOS Bull Smart Burst Buffer and IO-instrumentation
text.text: Moving to Bull Echirolles I joined a team developping a smart burst buffer and IO instrumentation tool for clusters. This is currently a commercial product you can look on the ATOS website.
